# elizaOS Tweet Ideas

## 1) Tweet Ideas (10-20)

1. prod auth is brittle until it is boring. steward oauth is now pkce + signed proxy calls. next: secrets + redirect allowlists so “sign in with eliza cloud” never 400s again. #AI #OperatingSystem #Innovation

2. scoped token minting shipped: agent tokens can be cut with explicit `trade:order` capability, not ambient power. permissions should read like code. #AI #OperatingSystem #Innovation

3. new container management routes are live in cloud api. deploy, read, quota. infra that can be reasoned about by agents and humans. #AI #OperatingSystem #Innovation

4. cors policy updates for third-party apps: idempotency-key and affiliate headers are first-class. browser preflight is a contract, not an obstacle. #AI #OperatingSystem #Innovation

5. autonomous goal loops for monetized apps: `/goal` can now run the build-monetized-app loop with capped self-spend. autonomy with explicit budgets. #AI #OperatingSystem #Innovation

6. execution mode is no longer implicit. shared vs dedicated is exposed in the app surface so workloads stop pretending they’re the same. #AI #OperatingSystem #Innovation

7. recovery controls landed in the agent management surface. failure isn’t a cliff; it’s a state machine with handles. #AI #OperatingSystem #Innovation

8. ui pass: liquid-glass motion on the continuous chat overlay. fewer edges. more signal. the shell should feel like a stable instrument. #AI #OperatingSystem #Innovation

9. windows e2e is unblocked. env-specific boot issues got shaved down so cross-platform regressions surface early, not in prod. #AI #OperatingSystem #Innovation

10. voice-to-entity binding bug closed: plugin-local-inference route registration was the missing wire. sometimes “ai” is just a path. #AI #OperatingSystem #Innovation

11. learning in a down market: contributors are showing up anyway. curiosity beats urgency; llms turn overload into a queue. #AI #OperatingSystem #Innovation

12. onboarding note: if you feel both early and late, you’re in the right repo. start in coders, ask one precise question, ship one small fix. #AI #OperatingSystem #Innovation

13. security posture: prompt-injection defense and execution-boundary audits are becoming table stakes for autonomous agents that can sign things. #AI #OperatingSystem #Innovation

14. cloud billing truth needs a single ledger: app-credits vs org-balance mismatches block seamless spend. money is a data model. #AI #OperatingSystem #Innovation

15. auth hardening without drift: when steward changes invariants (pkce, hmac), our sdk and frontend must upgrade in lockstep. contracts or outages. #AI #OperatingSystem #Innovation

16. community ops signal: scammer alerts are part of the runtime. trust is infrastructure; moderation is uptime. #AI #OperatingSystem #Innovation

## 2) Concise Twitter Thread (3-5 tweets maximum)

1. eliza cloud is in a hardening cycle: auth, permissions, routing. we want agent infra that fails loudly, recovers cleanly, and stays legible. #AI #OperatingSystem #Innovation

2. steward oauth now speaks pkce, and proxy calls are signed. scoped token minting is here (`trade:order`) so capabilities are explicit, not implied. #AI #OperatingSystem #Innovation

3. cloud api grew real ops surfaces: container deploy/read/quota routes, tighter cors for third-party apps, and reliability fixes that make browsers first-class clients. #AI #OperatingSystem #Innovation

4. autonomy moved forward: goal-driven loops for monetized apps with spend caps, shared vs dedicated execution modes, and recovery controls in the app surface. #AI #OperatingSystem #Innovation

5. in the community: markets down, curiosity up. new contributors are finding the coders channel, using llms to cut through overload, and shipping anyway. #AI #OperatingSystem #Innovation

## 3) Platform-specific Post

**elizaos (technical audience):**  
steward oauth drift caused prod login 400s; response is structural: pkce everywhere, signed proxy calls, scoped tokens, and container routes with auditable contracts. we’re building an agent os where auth and spend are composable primitives. #AI #OperatingSystem #Innovation

**auto.fun (crypto-native audience):**  
market’s cooked but the stack keeps shipping. pkce auth, signed requests, scoped `trade:order` tokens, and agents that can run the monetized loop with capped self-spend. fewer vibes, more permissions. deploy or die. #AI #OperatingSystem #Innovation

Eliza Cloud Infrastructure and Agent Autonomy Update - June 5, 2026
---
Development on June 5, 2026 centered on hardening Eliza Cloud infrastructure and advancing agent autonomy features. A production outage is currently active, with login failures caused by OAuth 400 errors affecting the Sign in with Eliza Cloud flow. This requires immediate coordination to deploy signing secrets and update tenant redirect allowlists. Additionally, a ledger mismatch between app-credits and org-balance is blocking seamless spending and awaiting an architectural resolution.

On the security front, the team implemented PKCE for Steward OAuth, added request signing for Steward proxy calls, and introduced scoped token minting with specific trade:order permissions. API reliability was improved through new container management routes and updated CORS policies for third-party app integrations.

Agent autonomy saw meaningful progress, with autonomous goal-driven loops now enabled for monetized applications, allowing agents to self-manage spending caps. Agent management was also improved by exposing execution modes such as Shared versus Dedicated and integrating recovery controls into the app surface.

On the developer experience side, the UI received a liquid-glass motion styling pass, Cloud SDK affordances were refined, and environment-specific issues blocking Windows E2E test execution were resolved. A previously reported voice-to-entity binding failure was closed after correctly registering routes for the plugin-local-inference pipeline.

Work still in progress includes keeping local first-run selection local, adding a third-party plugin registry entry for plugin-madeonsol, and building out smoke tests for the monetized-app loop on the cloud mock environment.

# Overall Project Weekly Summary (May 31 - 6, 2026)

## Executive Summary
This week, the ElizaOS project focused on maturing our cloud infrastructure and enhancing the reliability of our autonomous AI agents. By improving how agents are billed, managed, and tasked, we have taken a significant step toward a more stable and professional-grade framework for both Web2 and Web3 users.

### Key Strategic Initiatives & Outcomes

**Strengthening Cloud Infrastructure and Reliability**
_Goal: We aimed to create a more professional, scalable environment for hosting AI agents._
- We introduced automated billing and usage tracking in [elizaos/eliza](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza), allowing for clearer management of agent costs.
- We improved agent security and stability by isolating them into dedicated, private containers ([elizaos/eliza](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza)).

**Enhancing Agent Intelligence and Autonomy**
_Goal: We wanted our agents to be more effective at completing complex tasks without needing constant guidance._
- We launched a new coding workbench in [elizaos/eliza](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza) that allows agents to write and edit code more efficiently.
- We refined the "brain" of our agents to better prioritize important tasks, such as coding, over simple web searches ([elizaos/eliza](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza)).

**Improving the User and Developer Experience**
_Goal: We focused on making the framework faster to start and easier for new developers to learn._
- We optimized the desktop application in [elizaos/eliza](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza) to launch faster by preparing background tasks more efficiently.
- We expanded our documentation and added helpful prompts to the chat interface to make it easier for new users to get started ([elizaos/eliza](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza)).

## Repository Spotlights

### elizaos/eliza
- Implemented metered billing ([elizaos/eliza#8094](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8094)) and per-inference spend tracking ([elizaos/eliza#8089](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8089)) to support a mature cloud-hosted model.
- Isolated agents into dedicated containers via the sandbox registry ([elizaos/eliza#8073](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8073)).
- Launched a Claude-Code-style workbench to support streaming and code diffs ([elizaos/eliza#8095](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8095)).
- Optimized desktop startup performance through deferred model warmup ([elizaos/eliza#8151](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8151), [elizaos/eliza#8148](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8148)).
- Expanded Storybook documentation and added prompt suggestions to the chat overlay to improve onboarding ([elizaos/eliza#8200](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8200), [elizaos/eliza#8201](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8201)).
- Resolved critical build failures and agent booting regressions ([elizaos/eliza#8099](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8099), [elizaos/eliza#8100](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8100)).
- Fixed JSON-RPC error reporting and logic issues that previously hindered sub-agent performance ([elizaos/eliza#8101](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8101), [elizaos/eliza#8127](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8127)).

## Executive Summary
May 2026 was a month of foundational hardening for ElizaOS, focused on stabilizing our core framework and ensuring seamless compatibility across diverse computing environments. By resolving critical infrastructure bottlenecks and standardizing how our plugins interact with the core system, we have significantly improved the reliability and security of our AI agent deployments.

### Key Strategic Initiatives & Outcomes

**Strengthening Core Reliability and Cross-Platform Stability**
*Goal: To ensure our agents run consistently and securely on any server or operating system.*
- We hardened the agent runtime for headless environments, preventing system crashes on Linux and Windows servers ([elizaos/eliza](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza)).
- We implemented a new, secure way to manage sensitive information across different platforms, making it easier to integrate with our settings interface ([elizaos/eliza](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza)).
- We resolved complex system-level errors that previously hindered reliable agent performance in headless deployments ([elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow)).

**Modernizing Our Build Architecture**
*Goal: To align our plugin ecosystem with modern web standards for better performance and easier maintenance.*
- We transitioned multiple plugins ([plugin-ollama](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-ollama), [plugin-openrouter](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-openrouter), [plugin-pdf](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-pdf), and [plugin-openai](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-openai)) to a modern, modular build structure, eliminating runtime conflicts with the core framework.

**Ensuring Secure and Reliable Integrations**
*Goal: To maintain high security and service continuity for our decentralized exchange and communication tools.*
- We migrated our Jupiter integration to the official API endpoint and implemented mandatory security key authentication to keep our decentralized trading features compliant and reliable ([elizaos-plugins/plugin-jupiter](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-jupiter)).
- We refined configuration management for Telegram and Discord plugins to ensure that agent settings are applied correctly and consistently during runtime ([elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram), [elizaos-plugins/plugin-discord](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-discord)).

### Cross-Repository Coordination
- **Shared Framework Alignment**: A major project-wide effort was undertaken to align all plugins with the ESM-first architecture of the core framework. By removing legacy code formats across multiple repositories, we ensured that the entire ElizaOS ecosystem remains compatible, modular, and free of runtime errors.
- **Unified Runtime Fixes**: Coordination between the core framework and the n8n-workflow plugin allowed us to resolve deep-seated issues regarding authentication and message handling, ensuring that user settings propagate correctly from the dashboard to the agent runtime.

## Repository Spotlights

### elizaos/eliza
- Hardened the runtime for headless servers by bypassing unavailable system secret services ([#7230](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7230)).
- Introduced `@elizaos/vault` to enable secure, cross-platform secrets management ([#7197](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7197)).
- Fixed critical authentication regressions, including SIWE failures and bot token bridging ([#7288](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7288), [#7327](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7327)).
- Improved user interaction by adding clarification workflows for n8n and better context integration for Discord ([#7316](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7316), [#7315](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7315)).

### elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow
- Formalized how agents request missing information from users by implementing `ClarificationRequest` structures ([#27](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow/pull/27)).
- Improved prompt routing reliability by switching to ID-based directives ([#28](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow/pull/28)).
- Resolved critical runtime crashes and message loss issues that affected Telegram bot polling ([#7244](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow/issues/7244), [#7245](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow/issues/7245)).

### elizaos-plugins/plugin-jupiter
- Successfully migrated to the official `api.jup.ag` endpoint to ensure service continuity ([#4](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-jupiter/pull/4)).
- Implemented mandatory API key authentication to meet modern security standards for swap requests ([#4](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-jupiter/pull/4)).

### elizaos-plugins/plugin-discord
- Addressed configuration inconsistencies where voice modules bypassed standard auto-reply settings ([#49](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-discord/issues/49), [#50](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-discord/pull/50)).

### elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram
- Fixed a configuration issue to ensure Telegram setup tokens are correctly applied during runtime execution ([#29](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram/pull/29)).

      "body": "## Summary\n\nWhen the planner narrates a coding sub-agent's `task_complete` back to the user, the **deliverable** (the printed value / command output) is frequently dropped, truncated, or buried under process chatter — while the underlying sub-agent work is correct. This is a *presentation* defect, not a capability one.\n\nEvidence: a live 48-prompt sweep (30-scenario battery + smart/normie/harden suites) across all three backends found **0 capability-broken defects** — every answer was correct, every spawn produced its artifact, every safety probe handled. The **only** remaining defect class is this narration-envelope.\n\n## Manifestations (all observed live)\n\n1. **Deliverable drop on combo / printed-value tasks.** On the no-change-set completion path, `composeNarration` → `stripToolTranscript(response)` (`sub-agent-router.ts`) deletes the entire `[tool output: …] … [/tool output]` block. For a \"print a value / run a command and report the output\" task (no files changed → no diff), that block *is* the deliverable, so it's gone before the user sees it.\n2. **Value truncation by the parent model.** The synthetic `task_complete` memory is re-rendered by the parent `RESPONSE_HANDLER` (a weak planner in some deployments), which truncates the value — e.g. `2026-06-02` → `202`, a BTC price `70234` → `705`. Per-backend signature: opencode truncates, codex emits verbose process narration + `Completed <task>` banners, claude is cleanest.\n3. **Stale retry attempts leak.** When a first attempt errors (e.g. `session_state_lost` / an ACP crash) and a respawn recovers, the user sees *both* `\"the sub-agent was blocked / crashed, let me try again\"` **and** the eventual deliverable.\n4. **Process chatter.** Sub-agents (esp. codex) narrate their steps (`\"I'll load the workspace context first\"`, `\"rg is not installed\"`, `\"reverse.py already exists\"`); the parent relays it.\n\n## Proposed structural fixes\n\n**(a) Carry the printed-value deliverable verbatim (fixes #1, mitigates #2).** A structural carrier that mirrors the existing verified-URL carrier:\n- *Producer* (`sub-agent-router.ts`): on the `task_complete && !changeSet && verifiedUrls.length === 0` path, recover the captured stdout from the **raw** `response` (before `composeNarration` strips it) via the inverse of `stripToolTranscript`, and stamp it as `metadata.subAgentDeliverable` (gated to a short single block, ≤2KB, so multi-KB transcripts stay on the model-rendered path). *(I have a ready, tested producer patch for this.)*\n- *Consumer* (`evaluators/sub-agent-completion.ts`): when `subAgentDeliverable` is present, relay it **verbatim** instead of letting the model re-summarize/truncate it — parallel to how `subAgentVerifiedUrls` is already consumed.\n\n**(b) Terminal-reply filter (fixes #3, #4).** Emit only the *terminal* sub-agent reply; suppress stale `blocked`/`failed`/`state_lost` attempts that a later retry superseded, plus raw tool chatter and `Completed <task>` banners. **Caveat — needs care:** the `session_state_lost` error event is currently *coupled to the respawn trigger* (re-injecting it is what prompts the parent to respawn), so suppressing the user-facing narration requires an internal-only routing path that keeps the respawn signal. This is the part that needs design discussion.\n\n**(c) Already shipped — source-side mitigation.** #8158 adds an output-discipline section to the scaffolded sub-agent operating manual (\"lead with the deliverable, don't narrate process, no `Completed <task>` banner, a bare workspace has no SOUL.md/USER.md — don't hunt for them\"), which measurably reduced #4 at the source (codex re-probes went from a verbose banner to a bare `5050` / `2026-06-02`).\n\n## Coordination note\n\n`sub-agent-router.ts` and `evaluators/sub-agent-completion.ts` were recently rewritten on `develop`; this should be coordinated with that work rather than landed blind. Happy to open the (a)-producer + (a)-consumer as a focused PR if the approach looks right.",

      "id": "I_kwDOMT5cIs8AAAABEDmEnQ",
      "title": "app-core dist/entry.js externalizes un-hoisted transitive deps (jose, @node-rs/argon2) → ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND outside Bun's loader",
      "author": "lalalune",
      "number": 8142,
      "repository": "elizaos/eliza",
      "body": "## Summary\n\n`packages/app-core`'s built `dist/entry.js` imports transitive runtime deps (`jose`, `@node-rs/argon2`, and likely more) as **external** packages. These deps live in Bun's content-addressable store but are not linked at the top level where Node-style resolution looks, so any command that runs `dist/entry.js` outside Bun's workspace-aware loader fails with `Cannot find module`.\n\nSurfaced by a downstream QA smoke pass (milady-ai/milady#2179, finding #1 \"layer 2\").\n\n## Repro\n\n1. Build the app-core entry (`dist/entry.js`, ~7.6 MB across ~20 chunks).\n2. Run it with a plain Node-style loader (e.g. via `doctor`, or any path that doesn't go through Bun's workspace loader).\n3. First failure: `Cannot find module 'jose'`. Manually linking `jose` from Bun's store unblocks it, then it immediately hits `Cannot find module '@node-rs/argon2'`, and so on — whack-a-mole suggesting dozens more.\n\n## Root cause\n\nThe bundler (tsdown) marks these runtime deps `external`, but they are transitive (not hoisted to the top-level `node_modules`), so Node-style resolution from `dist/entry.js` can't find them.\n\n## Proposed fixes (pick one)\n\n- (a) Bundle the needed transitive runtime deps **inline** in `dist/entry.js` (tsdown config: move them out of `external`).\n- (b) Declare all needed runtime deps as **direct deps** of the package so they hoist to the top level.\n- (c) Ship a runtime/loader that knows how to resolve into Bun's store.\n\n## Severity\n\nMedium — affects any command that runs `dist/entry.js` outside Bun's loader (e.g. `doctor`), and is broader than a single command.\n",

      "id": "I_kwDOMT5cIs8AAAABEhtd5g",
      "title": "Steward login broken in prod: OAuth authorize 400 (code_challenge) + email/send 400 — blocks all Sign in with Eliza Cloud",
      "author": "NubsCarson",
      "number": 8257,
      "repository": "elizaos/eliza",
      "body": "## Summary\nSteward login is broken in production — **both** email and Google OAuth fail with 400 — which blocks \"Sign in with Eliza Cloud\" for **every** app (the cli-login flow bounces all third-party apps + the dashboard through this same login).\n\nReproduces with plain `curl` (no browser, no extensions), so it's a server-side prod issue, not a client environment.\n\n## Symptoms (prod, `api.elizacloud.ai`)\n**Google/OAuth** — `GET /steward/auth/oauth/google/authorize?...&response_type=code`:\n```\n{\"ok\":false,\"error\":\"code_challenge is required for response_type=code\"}\n```\nAdding `code_challenge` + `code_challenge_method=S256` still returns **400**.\n\n**Email** — `POST /steward/auth/email/send` → **400 Bad Request** (from `steward-login-section.tsx` → `signInWithEmail`).\n\n## Root cause (the OAuth half)\nThe Steward `/authorize` endpoint now **requires PKCE** (`code_challenge`), but:\n- `packages/cloud-frontend/src/pages/login/steward-oauth-url.ts` → `buildStewardOAuthAuthorizeUrl()` builds the URL with `response_type: \"code\"` and **no** `code_challenge` / `code_challenge_method`.\n- The exchange flow is **nonce-based with no verifier**: `packages/cloud-api/auth/steward-nonce-exchange/route.ts` documents *\"The Steward API authenticates the exchange purely by possession of the one-time `code` — there is no client [verifier].\"*\n\nSo the authorize service (PKCE-required) and the cloud login flow (nonce-based, no PKCE) are out of sync — and even a hand-added `code_challenge` still 400s, suggesting a deeper Steward auth misconfig/regression in prod.\n\n## Impact\nNo one can sign in via Eliza Cloud right now — dashboard, edad, and every third-party app using the cli-login \"Sign in with Eliza Cloud\" bounce here and dead-end at this login.\n\n## Fix options\n- **A (restore parity):** make Steward `/authorize` accept the nonce flow **without** `code_challenge` (matches the existing nonce-exchange contract). Simplest if PKCE was enabled unintentionally.\n- **B (full PKCE):** frontend generates a `code_verifier` + S256 `code_challenge`, sends `code_challenge` at `/authorize` and stores the verifier; `steward-nonce-exchange` forwards `code_verifier` to `/auth/oauth/exchange`.\n\nThe `email/send` 400 handler isn't in this monorepo (Steward service) — flag for that team too; it may share the same prod regression.\n\nFound via the cli-login flow from a third-party browser app.\n",

When `secrets: inherit` can't supply a `required: true` secret, GitHub fails the parent at startup.\n\nConfirmed via `gh secret list -R elizaOS/eliza` that these are missing:\n\n| Secret | Required by `APPLE_ID` | `release-electrobun.yml`, `apple-store-release.yml` | notarization Apple ID |\n| `APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD` | `release-electrobun.yml`, `apple-store-release.yml` | notarization |\n| `APPLE_TEAM_ID` | `release-electrobun.yml`, `apple-store-release.yml` | Apple Developer Team ID |\n| `ITC_TEAM_ID` | `apple-store-release.yml` | App Store Connect team ID |\n| `APP_STORE_APP_ID` | `apple-store-release.yml` | numeric app ID |\n| `MATCH_PASSWORD` | `apple-store-release.yml` | Fastlane Match passphrase |\n| `MATCH_GIT_URL` | `apple-store-release.yml` | Fastlane Match repo |\n| `WINDOWS_SIGN_CERT_BASE64` | `windows-store-release.yml` | Windows code-signing cert |\n| `WINDOWS_SIGN_CERT_PASSWORD` | `windows-store-release.yml` | cert password |\n| `WINDOWS_SIGN_TIMESTAMP_URL` | The leaf workflows already self-guard most calls.\n3. **Stop calling unconfigured publish workflows** from `release-all.yml` until their secrets exist.\n\nProbably want a mix: add Debian GPG (lowest-risk, internal-only) and Apple signing (highest-value), and gate Windows Store + Flathub behind explicit opt-in until those credentials are arranged.\n\n## Context\n\n- Tag for `v2.0.3` is live on `main` (`fa240156ed`). All other release workflows (NPM, Linux ISO, Android, Snap, Electrobun, etc.) ran or are running normally.\n- Failed runs are also reproducible on the prior `v2.0.2` attempt — this is the pre-existing block, not a new regression.\n- I don't have admin rights to download the run logs, which would confirm the exact secret-validation error message.       "id": "I_kwDOMT5cIs8AAAABDWGTpw",
      "title": "desktop-ffi: prewarm violates GGML_ASSERT(n_tokens_all <= cparams.n_batch) on eliza-1-0_8b after shim loads",
      "author": "NubsCarson",
      "number": 7991,
      "repository": "elizaos/eliza",
      "body": "## context\n\nafter #7988 lands the windows-x86_64 ffi build path, `libeliza-llama-shim.dll` loads into `bun:ffi` with all 39 symbols resolved. the very next layer — prewarm — crashes with a ggml batch-sizing assert on `eliza-1-0_8b` text bundle.\n\n## crash\n\n```\nGGML_ASSERT(n_tokens_all <= cparams.n_batch) failed\n```\n\nrepro: `~/.eliza` already has the `eliza-1-0_8b-128k.gguf` bundle staged; on first activation the prewarm batch builder asks the runtime to decode more tokens than the context's `n_batch` slot allows. fatal.\n\n## environment\n\n- platform: windows-x86_64\n- backend: cuda (cuda 12.4, msvc, ninja, llama.cpp pinned to the ref in `build-llama-cpp-desktop-dylib.mjs`)\n- shim: built from `eliza_llama_shim.c` with the stub set added in #7988\n- bundle: `bundles/0_8b/text/eliza-1-0_8b-128k.gguf` from default-user `~/.eliza/local-inference/`\n- runtime: `desktop-llama-adapter.ts` → `eliza_llama_decode_unified` path\n\n## what i think is going on\n\nthe prewarm path appears to construct a batch that targets the manifest's declared context window (128k tokens) without first clamping against the actual `cparams.n_batch` value the ffi context was created with. on linux/macos this might be masked by larger default `n_batch` or different prewarm sizing; on windows-cuda the assert fires.\n\ncandidates (i don't want to put words in anyone's mouth):\n\n1. prewarm should size the batch off `cparams.n_batch`, not `n_ctx`.\n2. context creation on the desktop adapter should set `n_batch >= prewarm_tokens` for the chosen bundle.\n3. the bundle manifest's recommended prewarm token count should be exposed and used as both inputs.\n\n## scope\n\n- not blocked by anything outstanding once #7988 lands.\n- separate from the build-time fixes in #7988 (which are infrastructure) — this is a runtime sizing bug one layer down.\n- related context PR: #7983 (local-inference dflash discovery + runtime router wiring).\n",

      "number": 8180,
      "body": "## Summary\n\nAdds a full **chat-shell UI for the multi-agent orchestrator**, modeled on the\n[odysseus](https://github.com/pewdiepie-archdaemon/odysseus) frontend (MIT) and\nrebuilt from scratch in **React on `@elizaos/ui`**, wired to the **existing\nelizaOS runtime**. This is **frontend only** — there is no new backend; all\ntask/session state continues to live in `@elizaos/plugin-agent-orchestrator`.\n\nThe new surface renders at **`/odysseus`** (and the existing **`/orchestrator`**\nroute) as a full-window app: a labeled session sidebar, a rich composer, a theme\nengine, the memory/skills/notes/settings panels, and the full set of tool views.\n\n> Credit: the original UI/UX design is from **odysseus** (MIT). This is an\n> independent React reimplementation on elizaOS's own stack — none of the\n> odysseus Python backend is used.\n\n## What's included\n\n**New `plugins/plugin-task-coordinator/src/odysseus/` shell (~40 files):**\n- `OdysseusShell` root + `SessionSidebar` (folders, multi-select, pin, drag,\n  resize) + collapsible 48px `IconRail` (mutually exclusive with the sidebar)\n- `Composer` with slash-command + emoji autocomplete and a bottom action row\n- Theme engine (`ThemeMenu` / `PresetsPanel`): preset recolor + customize\n  (font / density / background), mapped onto elizaOS semantic CSS vars\n- `MemoryPanel` (memories/skills/add/settings), `SkillsPanel`, `NotesPanel`,\n  `SettingsPanel`\n- Tool views: tasks, models, email, calendar, gallery (+ editor), compare,\n  research, voice, group chat, cookbook, document library, admin\n- A cross-view **window manager** (`WindowManager` + `useWindowControls`):\n  drag / resize / edge-snap / minimize-to-dock (`MinimizedDock`)\n- `useKeyboardShortcuts`, `useEscapeClose`, `useTaskRoom`, `useChatSubmit`\n\n**Orchestrator render helpers:**\n- `orchestrator-markdown` (via `marked`), `orchestrator-plan`,\n  `orchestrator-reasoning`\n- `orchestrator-stream` now always renders the user's own `stdin` messages\n  (preserves the existing `senderKind === \"user\"` fix)\n\n**App-shell `fullBleed` capability (`packages/ui`):**\n- Opt-in `fullBleed?: boolean` on `AppShellPageRegistration`\n  (backward-compatible). When set, the host shell mounts the page edge-to-edge\n  via `ViewRouter` — **no header, no tab bar, no routed padding** — so a view\n  that owns its whole window has no surrounding dashboard chrome.\n- This **replaces the prior hardcoded `tab !== \"orchestrator\"` header\n  special-case** in `RoutedShellContent` with a general, declarative flag.\n- `/orchestrator` and the new `/odysseus` route are registered `fullBleed: true`.\n\n**Other:**\n- adds the `marked` dependency to the plugin\n- isolates each `*.live.e2e.test.ts` suite's default chromium profile dir so\n  parallel vitest file execution can't collide on the ProcessSingleton lock\n\n## Verification\n\nBuilt on a fresh branch off current `develop` and verified there:\n\n- biome check — clean across all 53 changed source files\n- `tsgo --noEmit` (plugin) — **0 errors** against current `develop` APIs\n- `tsgo --noEmit` (`@elizaos/ui`) — 0 errors in `App.tsx` / `app-shell-registry.ts`\n- plugin unit tests — **19/19** pass\n- plugin build — `tsup` + views bundle + type declarations all succeed\n- live e2e (`*.live.e2e.test.ts`) — **26/26** pass against a running dev stack,\n  and Playwright render checks confirm the host header is absent on `/odysseus`\n  (full-bleed) and the global chat overlay behaves correctly\n\n## Scope / notes\n\n- **Frontend only.** No actions/providers/services added; state stays in\n  `@elizaos/plugin-agent-orchestrator`.\n- The `fullBleed` flag is opt-in and changes no existing page's behavior.\n- Some per-view actions render honest empty/disabled states where elizaOS has no\n  corresponding client method yet (e.g. email IMAP/SMTP, calendar CalDAV,\n  gallery image backend, voice TTS/STT) — these light up unchanged once the\n  matching runtime routes exist.
